code:
def min_product_subset(arr):
    n = len(arr)
    if n == 0:
        return 0

    neg_count = 0
    pos_count = 0
    zero_count = 0
    prod_nonzero = 1
    max_neg = float('-inf')  # Stores the largest negative number (closest to zero)
    min_pos = float('inf')   # Stores the smallest positive number

    for num in arr:
        if num < 0:
            neg_count += 1
            prod_nonzero *= num
            max_neg = max(max_neg, num)
        elif num > 0:
            pos_count += 1
            prod_nonzero *= num
            min_pos = min(min_pos, num)
        else:  # num == 0
            zero_count += 1

    # Case 1: No negative numbers in the array
    if neg_count == 0:
        if zero_count > 0:
            # If there are zeros, the minimum product can be 0 (by including a zero)
            return 0
        else:
            # All numbers are positive. The minimum product is the smallest positive number.
            return min_pos
    
    # Case 2: At least one negative number in the array
    else: # neg_count > 0
        if neg_count % 2 == 1:
            # Odd number of negative numbers.
            # The product of all non-zero numbers will be negative.
            # This is the most negative product we can get.
            return prod_nonzero
        else:  # neg_count % 2 == 0 (Even number of negative numbers)
            # The product of all non-zero numbers (prod_nonzero) will be positive.
            # To get a negative product, we must exclude one negative number.
            # To make the product most negative, we exclude the negative number
            # that contributes least to the magnitude (i.e., the largest negative number, max_neg).
            return prod_nonzero // max_neg
